titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Caddell to the Urban District Council, 1 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Caddell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Henry Caddell, a clerk at Dublin Castle, to the Longford Urban District Council concerning a surplus of money generated from the sale of licenses under the Dog Regulation (Ireland) Act 1865 in Longford for the year 1915.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Registrar of Petty Sessions Clerk's Office Dublin Castle. 1stJune 1916 Sir â I beg to acquaint you for the information
of the Urban District Council, that the proposition of
surplus monies arising from the sale of licences
under the 'Dogs regulation (Ireland) Act 1865
amounts to Â£10.19.10 for the Urban District of
Longford for the year 1915 and to state that the
Lord Lieutenant has directed that this sum
should be paid over to the Treasurer to the
Urban District Council purssuant to the
'Dog regulation (Ireland) Act 1865 to be
applied by him in aid of the Urban District rates
in such manner as the Council shall direct. I am Sir
your obd servt H Caddell The Clerk Urban Dh Council Longford.
